channel.title: AI Governance on Asymmetric Intelligence
channel.description: Recent content in AI Governance on Asymmetric Intelligence

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 16 April 2026
item.description: Governance momentum remains stalled this week with no frontier capability leaps, safety incidents, or regulatory milestones reported in provided documents. EU AI Act standards vacuum persists ahead of August 2026 high-risk obligations, while enterprise AI maturity lags per Digitopia 2026 report showing global DAIMI average 2.93 and AI maturity at 2.38 despite rapid capability advances. US DAIMI at 2.99 trails APAC adoption cycles highlighting reality gap beyond Silicon Valley pilots. Capability tier tracker flags T3 enterprise developments with medium risk from execution chasm in commercial scaling. No T1 frontier model releases or T2 advanced capability jumps noted; benchmark saturation discourse continues but lacks acute signals. Regulatory tracker confirms EU AI Act in force status with ongoing CEN-CENELEC drafts failing to close compliance gap. AI-FIMI layer silent with no observed capabilities in information operations. Autonomous systems and critical infrastructure show no new military AI or sector governance shifts. Key judgment centers on widening deployment maturity gap risking obsolescence of current frameworks for scaling AI beyond labs. Cross-monitor candidates minimal given null signals. Overall snapshot indicates amber frontier safety amid stable US-China policy gap.

item.title: AI Governance Monitor — W/E 1 April 2026
item.description: Anthropic’s double opsec failure in five days — Claude Mythos leaked via CMS and KAIROS/BUDDY/undercover-mode exposed via npm — reveals systemic internal controls failure at the lab with the largest DoD-adjacent AI footprint, exactly as the GSA ‘any lawful use’ clause threatens to codify no-safety-restrictions as standard federal AI procurement.

item.title: AI Governance Monitor — Week of 30 March 2026
item.description: China’s state formalisation of AI tokens as an economic settlement unit and OpenAI’s compute reallocation from video synthesis to robotics pre-training are this week’s two structural signals — geopolitical AI architecture is now being built in terminology committees as much as in data centers.

item.title: Methodology — AI Governance Monitor
item.description:
The AI Governance Monitor applies a consistent research standard across all 16 modules every week. This page documents that standard.

Source Hierarchy

All items are sourced according to a three-tier hierarchy. Tier 1 is always preferred; lower tiers are only used when no Tier 1 source exists.

Tier Sources Rule
T1 Lab research blogs, arXiv/bioRxiv preprints, official regulatory texts, court filings, government gazettes, official changelogs Always use. Link to the primary source directly — never to press coverage of it.
T2 Reuters, Bloomberg, FT, The Information, Import AI, AI Snake Oil, Lawfare, RAND, Brookings, Chatham House, IISS, RUSI Use only when no Tier 1 source exists.
T3 The Verge, Wired, TechCrunch, Ars Technica, general tech press Last resort only. Flagged: ⚠️ Tier 3 source — primary not found.

Signal Standard

An item is included if and only if:
